J-L1.description: the user mainly wants a conclusion, comparison, explanation, or review
J-L1.anti_shallow_bar: do not pretend a decision task is complete just because a guess sounds plausible

F-M1.description: something already broken must stop failing
F-M1.anti_shallow_bar: main failure path and one nearby regression check are both required

C-M1.description: a bounded capability inside an existing system must change
C-M1.anti_shallow_bar: do not count visible output alone as progress; the named gap must shrink

A-M1.description: the real job is to make the system truly able to do something deeper or less visible
A-M1.anti_shallow_bar: do not accept a shell that only looks complete; hidden rule or deeper ability must be proved

P-H1.description: the job is to define or build a wider product or subsystem surface
P-H1.anti_shallow_bar: do not accept minimum-only slices when the product promise or release bar says fuller depth is required
